hypothesis: Introducing an aromatic residue at position 5 and a hydrophobic residue at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific complementarity with ECL2/TM5 while avoiding off-target interactions with SSTR3/5.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is generating candidates with strong SSTR2 binding but insufficient stability and selectivity. A tighter ddG threshold and targeted mutagenesis at non-pharmacophore positions will improve both stability and selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: The current ddG threshold is too lenient, allowing candidates with weak binding. Tightening the ddG cutoff will filter out weaker binders and improve overall stability.

To improve selectivity, we must disrupt conserved-pocket contacts in off-targets (SSTR3/SSTR5) at non-pharmacophore positions. Focusing mutations on these positions will help reduce off-target binding while preserving key pharmacophore residues.
